description_long: This utility pole consists of two tall, slender wooden poles supporting a pair of sagging electrical wires suspended between them. Each pole is topped with a horizontal crossarm, which holds insulators and hardware for securing the wires. The crossarms are positioned near the top of the poles and are oriented horizontally, with metal brackets and insulator mounts visible. The wires are slightly drooping, indicating tension and realistic weight. The wood of the poles appears weathered, with a natural brown color, while the hardware and insulators are metallic gray. No transformers, streetlights, or identification tags are visible on the structure.
description: A pair of tall wooden utility poles with horizontal crossarms and two sagging electrical wires suspended between them, featuring metallic insulators and hardware.
description_view_0: Frontal view of a single utility pole showing the crossarm, insulators, and attached wires head-on.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both utility poles and the full span of the two sagging wires between them.
description_view_2: Rear view of a single utility pole, similar to the front, with visible crossarm and insulators.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing both poles and the wires, mirroring view 1 from the other direction.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Each pole has a single horizontal crossarm mounted near the top, with both crossarms at the same height and parallel to each other.
